title: system tests: fix a system test in proxy environment
body:
When we are using a proxy, 'podman build - basic test' will be failed on remote.
This test needs to add the '--http-proxy' option.

Signed-off-by: Toshiki Sonoda <sonoda.toshiki@fujitsu.com>

<details>
<summary>’podman build - basic test’ is failed</summary>

```
not ok 1 podman build - basic test
# (from function `die' in file helpers.bash, line 595,
#  from function `run_podman' in file helpers.bash, line 215,
#  in test file 070-build.bats, line 23)
#   `PODMAN_TIMEOUT=240 run_podman build -t build_test --format=docker $tmpdir' failed
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
# 7f1e2c9cfe17 testimage-working-container
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote rm -f 7f1e2c9cfe17
# 7f1e2c9cfe17
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
# quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 f5a99120db64
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote build -t build_test --format=docker /tmp/podman_bats.Le4P60/build-test
# STEP 1/3: FROM quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018
# STEP 2/3: RUN apk add nginx
# fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.16/main/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
# ERROR: https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.16/main: DNS lookup error
# WARNING: Ignoring https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.16/main: No such file or directory
# fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.16/community/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
# ERROR: https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.16/community: DNS lookup error
# WARNING: Ignoring https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.16/community: No such file or directory
# ERROR: unable to select packages:
#   nginx (no such package):
#     required by: world[nginx]
# Error: building at STEP "RUN apk add nginx": while running runtime: exit status 1
# [ rc=1 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #| FAIL: exit code is 1; expected 0
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote rm -t 0 -a -f
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote rmi -f build_test
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote image prune -f
# # [teardown]
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote pod rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote network prune --force
# # /usr/local/bin/podman-remote volume rm -a -f
```
</details>

comments.body:
I don't know anything about proxy environments, so I can't really review. What I'm curious about is why #15639, which also dealt with proxies, used `--http-proxy=false` but this PR uses just `--http-proxy`, which AIUI defaults to true anyway?

OOTO today and completely unreachable from this point on.

#15639 is the problem about the internal container-to-container networking.
This case doesn't need name resolution (--http-proxy=false).
On the other hand, this system test is the problem about the external networking.
This case needs name resolution (--http-proxy=true).

It is possible that this problem is due to a bug.
As shown below, `podman build` is successed.

<details>
<summary>Containerfile</summary>

```
# cat /root/test/Containerfile
FROM quay.io/libpod/testimage:20220615
RUN apk add nginx
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>podman build (Successed)</summary>

```
# podman build -t test /root/test

Executing busybox-1.35.0-r13.trigger
OK: 7 MiB in 17 packages
COMMIT test
--> dca0ed3d162
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest

```
</details>

However, `podman-remote build` is failed.
I think `podman-remote build` should be successed.

If this problem is a bug, I'll open a issue and fix it.
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL, this isn't something I can test or confirm or even look at.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc*, *rhatdan*, *sstosh* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/hold

There's some miscommunication going on here. Maybe it's on my end but we need to get it straightened out before this merges.

Based on @sstosh's last comment, I believe the problem is that **podman-remote is not defaulting to --http-proxy**. @sstosh, is that correct? If so, the solution is, as @sstosh indicated, to fix the bug. Merging this PR is the wrong thing to do.

Please correct me if I'm wrong.

I am so far out of my element here that I may just be talking nonsense... but I'm going to try anyway. My concern here is for end-user experience, so that's what I'll focus on.

* Scenario 1: podman-remote on my Linux desktop, with server in a datacenter in (somewhere far away). Sending my local proxy settings is probably anti-helpful.
* Scenario 2: podman-remote on my Linux desktop, server also on my Linux desktop.
* Scenario 3: podman-remote on Windows/Mac, server on a Linux VM: I think it makes sense here for podman-remote client to send proxy settings, because the VM is on the same Windows/Mac machine and using the same network?

Given that, and given the increasing popularity of Windows/Mac, I wonder if we could have a code path on Windows/Mac podman-remote that would explicitly pass through proxy settings?

Once again, I don't use Windows nor Macs nor proxies and really have no idea if what I'm saying makes sense. I'm just neurotic about making end-user experience a positive one.

@edsantiago  Yes, It is correct.
But, I don't know what is the best solution...

I believe the PROXY should be on the server side.
On MAC and WINDOWS, the podman machine should be setting those environment variables into the system and the `podman service` should be using them if they are set.
@n1hility or @mheon thoughts on what should be going on with the proxy settings?  See @edsantiago 's earlier comments.

> Given that, and given the increasing popularity of Windows/Mac, I wonder if we could have a code path on Windows/Mac podman-remote that would explicitly pass through proxy settings?

There’s already support for this for Mac and Windows: ´podman machine start´ looks for the common proxy env settings (http_proxy etc) and mirrors them in the guest so they are picked up by the service

It sounds like Macs and Windows are taken care of. This addition seems harmless in system-test context, so

/lgtm
